title: astragalus exscapus subsp. transsilvanicus
intro: Astragalus exscapus subsp. transsilvanicus, also known as Transylvanian milkvetch, is a plant species within the pea family Fabaceae, native to Central and Eastern Europe. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing immune function. Astragalus exscapus subsp. transsilvanicus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
